meta.musicNotes: Start from 4:30 into the track (skip alap, use jor/jhala crescendo). Drop to silence at scene 5 (prime reveal). Resume at scene 6.

scenes.narration: 43 लाख 20 हज़ार साल। ये संख्या धार्मिक नहीं है। ये गणित है। और NASA इससे सहमत है।
scenes.videoPrompt: Cinematic 4K dark space, camera slowly pushing through star field. A massive golden number '4,320,000' materialises from stardust particles, glowing with divine light. Cracks of golden energy spread from the number. No text, no people. Epic scale, Interstellar-style cinematography. Dark navy background with gold accents.

scenes.narration: इसे शनि की कक्षा से भाग दो — 1,46,568। पूर्ण। बृहस्पति — 3,64,220। पूर्ण। चन्द्रमा — 5 करोड़ 77 लाख। पूर्ण। बुध — 1 करोड़ 79 लाख। पूर्ण। नौ के नौ खगोलीय पिण्ड — एक भी शेष नहीं।
scenes.videoPrompt: Cinematic 4K solar system view. Saturn with magnificent rings rotating slowly, then Jupiter with swirling storms, then the Moon with craters lit by sunlight, then tiny Mercury near the blazing Sun. Each planet glows as if being 'counted'. Camera moves between them in smooth orbital transitions. Deep space background, volumetric lighting, photorealistic. No text.

scenes.narration: सटीकता? बुध — NASA से 66 सेकण्ड का अन्तर। शनि — साढ़े 29 साल की कक्षा में साढ़े 6 दिन। बिना दूरबीन। बिना उपग्रह। 1500 साल पहले।
scenes.videoPrompt: Split composition: left side shows an ancient Indian observatory at night — stone instruments, a scholar in white robes observing the sky with naked eyes, oil lamp flickering. Right side shows NASA mission control with glowing screens and satellite data. Both scenes lit dramatically. The two worlds existing simultaneously. Cinematic 4K, warm golden light on ancient side, cool blue on modern side.

scenes.narration: सात ग्रह 10 लाख 80 हज़ार साल में वापस आ जाते हैं। पर चन्द्रमा की गति बदलती रहती है — कभी 12 अंश प्रतिदिन, कभी 15। जब तक अपभू वापस नहीं आता — ये वही आकाश नहीं है।
scenes.videoPrompt: Cinematic 4K Moon in extreme close-up, surface details visible, moving across a star field. The Moon visibly speeds up and slows down in its orbit — stretching and compressing its path. The elliptical orbit is shown as a glowing golden trail behind it, clearly not circular. Earth visible in the background. Dramatic side-lighting creating deep shadows on lunar craters.

scenes.narration: [2 BEATS SILENCE] ... चन्द्रमा की अपभू का भगण है — 4 लाख 88 हज़ार 203। ... ये अभाज्य है। इसके गुणनखण्ड सिर्फ़ 1 और ये स्वयं हैं। 10 लाख 80 हज़ार में फिट नहीं होता। 21 लाख 60 हज़ार में नहीं। 32 लाख 40 हज़ार में नहीं। पहली संख्या जहाँ सब कुछ मिलता है — [BEAT] — 43 लाख 20 हज़ार।
scenes.videoPrompt: Dark void. A single point of golden light pulses in absolute darkness — alone, indivisible. Slowly, geometric patterns try to form around it — hexagons, grids, lattices — but each shatters on contact with the golden point. The point refuses to be divided. Finally, an enormous golden structure — like a cosmic mandala — descends and LOCKS perfectly around the point. Explosion of golden light filling the frame. Cinematic 4K, stark contrast, minimalist until the explosion.
scenes.audioDirection: SILENCE for first 2 seconds. Single tanpura drone from second 2. Build slowly. FULL CRESCENDO at 'जहाँ सब कुछ मिलता है'.

scenes.narration: और ये वही संख्या है जो युग दर्शन से निकलती है। 12 हज़ार दिव्य वर्ष गुणा 360 — बराबर 43 लाख 20 हज़ार। गणित और दर्शन — दो अलग रास्ते। एक ही उत्तर।
scenes.videoPrompt: Cinematic 4K Maha Vishnu reclining on Shesha Naga in the cosmic ocean. The serpent's coils form mathematical spirals. From Vishnu's navel, a golden lotus rises — and from the lotus, orbiting planets emerge as tiny golden spheres following elliptical paths. The cosmic ocean reflects starlight. Deep blues, rich golds, divine scale. Style of classical Indian temple art brought to photorealistic 3D life. No modern elements.

scenes.narration: 1500 साल पुराना कोड। आज भी दीवाली की तारीख़ इसी से निकलती है। आपकी कुण्डली इसी से बनती है। ये इतिहास नहीं — ये अभी चल रहा है।
scenes.videoPrompt: Cinematic 4K montage: A Hindu family lighting Diwali diyas at home, warm golden light. Cut to: a pandit's hands opening a traditional panchangam almanac, finger tracing a date. Cut to: a young woman checking her phone showing an astrology app with a kundali chart. Cut to: the golden number 4,320,000 floating in space, orbited by nine glowing celestial bodies. All scenes connected by golden thread-like light trails. Warm, emotional, intimate.

production_notes.sitar_track: Use sitar-darbari.mp3, start at ~4:30 (skip alap, enter at jor section for rhythmic energy). Mute sitar completely during scene 5 first 2 seconds. Bring back at scene 6 with full jhala crescendo.
production_notes.voiceover: edge-tts Hindi male voice. Slow and deliberate during scene 5 — each number gets its own beat. Fast and punchy during scenes 2-3.
production_notes.text_overlays: Gold text on semi-transparent dark strip at bottom. Devanagari font. Numbers in both Devanagari and Western numerals.
production_notes.estimated_cost: 7 clips × 8-15s × $0.40/s (Veo) ≈ $30-40. Use Kling ($0.28/s) to halve cost.
